commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c91c5cbb0fb35cc32c3ec98e4e0760bba316c566)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a warm `agy` process discovery/reuse path before the Antigravity CLI HTTPS spawn path and adds focused warm-reuse/fallback tests.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source and docs show current one-shot CLI fetches spawn and tear down `agy` with a 5s readiness deadline, and the PR body reports intermittent Linux failures, but there is no real warm-`agy` proof artifact.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling is needed because the contributor must provide real warm-`agy` proof and the remaining lifecycle fix must be reviewed before merge; automation cannot prove the reporter's Linux setup.

target.title: feat(antigravity): reuse a running agy language server for CLI usage
target.body:
## Problem

`codexbar usage --provider antigravity --source cli` spawns a fresh `agy` language
server on every one-shot invocation. A fresh server binds its port quickly, but
`GetUserStatus` returns transient initialization failures for a few seconds after the
port appears — and the readiness deadline is 5s. On Linux these one-shot calls
intermittently miss that window (~30% return "endpoint not ready" in my cron-based
Antigravity quota polling).

When CodexBar runs long-lived (the app, or `codexbar serve`), the warm session is
reused and this doesn't happen — the gap is specific to the one-shot CLI path.

## Solution

Before spawning, discover an already-running authenticated `agy` CLI language server
via the existing `detectProcessInfos(.ideAndCLI)` and reuse its listening ports through
the token-less `cliHTTPS` endpoint. If no warm server is found — or none answers with
parseable usage — fall through to the existing spawn path unchanged.

- IDE/app servers require a CSRF token and are excluded; only the `agy` CLI's
  token-less server is reused.
- The discovered process is externally owned, so the warm path intentionally leaves
  session lifecycle (`beginProbe`/`finishProbe`/reset) untouched — documented inline.
- "Warm if present, otherwise current behavior" — it never assumes a warm server exists.

The 5s deadline and the spawn path are unchanged; the spawn body was pulled into
`fetchBySpawning` in a separate refactor commit so the feature commit is purely additive.

## Tests

`Tests/CodexBarTests/AntigravityWarmAgyReuseTests.swift` (7 cases): warm discovered →
reused without spawning, IDE process excluded, no-warm and fetch-failure → spawn
fallback, and the `fetchUsingWarmSession` branch covered through the `spawnFetch` seam.
All driven by injected stubs (no live `agy`, no keychain).

`swift test --filter Antigravity` (208 pass) and `make check` (swiftlint --strict +
swiftformat) clean.

## Note

The reuse path goes through the same `fetchFromPorts`/`cliHTTPS` endpoint the spawn
path already uses, so authentication holds in the unit tests. I haven't confirmed the
~30%→0% improvement end-to-end against a real warm `agy` on Linux (no Linux box with
`agy` on hand) — left for CI (ubuntu) and real-world follow-up.
target.author: junmo-kim

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
